document_info.prepared_by: Patricia Nowak, Senior Broker, Steel City Insurance Group

business_description.nature_of_business: General contracting for commercial and institutional construction — healthcare facilities, educational buildings, and corporate offices throughout Western Pennsylvania

coverages_discussed.notes: Coverage requested for approximately 425 employees across all operations.
Occurrence form, including standard premises/operations and products/completed operations.
Coverage for 15 vehicles including hired and non-owned auto.
Coverage for all scheduled locations on a special form with replacement cost valuation.

positives: Formal return-to-work program that has reduced lost-time claims by over 30%.
Dedicated safety committee that meets monthly and conducts quarterly site audits.
Significant investment in employee safety training, with all field personnel OSHA-certified.
Formal business continuity and disaster recovery plan tested annually.
ISO 9001 certification, reflecting a commitment to quality and process control.
Contractual risk transfer provisions in all subcontractor and vendor agreements.
Annual background checks and MVR reviews for all employees in safety-sensitive positions.
425 employees with a structured HR program, low turnover and consistent safety training.
42 years of continuous operations under consistent management demonstrates operational stability.
concerns: Account experienced a cancellation in 2022-2023 with Zurich Insurance Group (Reason: Carrier non-renewed — book of business exit).
18 claims currently remain open out of 52 total claims.
Claims history warrants review; management has implemented corrective measures to address identified exposures.
risk_highlights.loss_history_summary: 52 total claims with $1,315,756.45 in total paid losses and $1,485,203.85 total incurred. 18 claims open, 34 closed. Annualized loss ratio is 18.7% (avg annual incurred $297,040.77 against current annual premium $1,589,164.70 over 5 loss years). Breakdown: Workers' Compensation - 35 claims, $1,126,509.37 paid, $1,171,607.20 incurred; General Liability - 8 claims, $105,420.70 paid, $176,946.61 incurred; Commercial Auto - 8 claims, $47,076.38 paid, $99,900.04 incurred; Commercial Property - 1 claim, $36,750.00 paid, $36,750.00 incurred.

extracted_text_sections.executive_summary: We are pleased to submit Keystone Commercial Builders, Inc. for your review and consideration. The insured is a general contracting for commercial and institutional construction — healthcare facilities, educational buildings, and corporate offices throughout Western Pennsylvania, headquartered in Pittsburgh, with approximately 42 years of operating history. Coverage is requested for Workers' Compensation, General Liability, Commercial Auto, Commercial Property.
extracted_text_sections.operations_description: General contracting for commercial and institutional construction — healthcare facilities, educational buildings, and corporate offices throughout Western Pennsylvania, headquartered in Pittsburgh, with approximately 42 years of operating history.
extracted_text_sections.safety_programs: The insured has implemented a formal return-to-work program that has reduced lost-time claims by over 30%. The insured maintains a dedicated safety committee that meets monthly and conducts quarterly site audits. Management has invested significantly in employee safety training, with all field personnel OSHA-certified. Background checks and MVR reviews are conducted annually for all employees in safety-sensitive positions.
extracted_text_sections.claims_narrative: The claims history warrants review; management has implemented corrective measures to address identified exposures. Over the review period, the insured has incurred 52 total claims with $1,315,756.45 in total paid losses and $1,485,203.85 total incurred. Currently, 18 claims remain open and 34 have been closed. Annualized loss ratio is 18.7% (avg annual incurred $297,040.77 against current annual premium $1,589,164.70 over 5 loss years). Note: account experienced a cancellation in 2022-2023 with Zurich Insurance Group. Reason: Carrier non-renewed — book of business exit.
